title: Generate GestureHotkeyApp Windows installer
objective: Build the current GestureHotkeyApp source and generate the Windows NSIS installer executable.

allowed_actions: Check required local tools: node, npm, cargo, rustc, git, cl, link, makensis if available.
Run npm install if node_modules is missing or dependencies are not installed.
Run npm run tauri build, preferably through artifacts/run-tauri-build.cmd when Visual Studio Build Tools environment is required.
Verify generated files under src-tauri/target/release and src-tauri/target/release/bundle/nsis.
Create report.json at repository root with the final result.
disallowed_actions: Do not redesign or refactor application code.
Do not change product requirements or shared AI rules.
Do not commit generated target/ output unless explicitly instructed later.
Do not upload installers to GitHub Releases or external services.
Do not enable action label overlay. It is intentionally disabled for stability.

expected_outputs.note: If this alias is not generated automatically, report that fact. Do not invent success.

verification: Confirm each required output file exists.
Record file size and last modified timestamp for each generated executable.
If possible, run the executable only far enough to confirm process startup; do not perform broad manual UI testing in this task.
Do not run the installer unless explicitly allowed by the human operator during this task.
stop_conditions: Required build tool is missing and cannot be installed without human action.
Installer output is not generated after a successful build.
Any step requires administrator permission or external credential approval.
